hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 5, A. Your opponent raised before the cards were dealt. You called.
Flop — Queen, King, 9. Your opponent bet. You called.
River — 6. Your opponent fired a large river bet.

wrongReasons: A 6 plays similarly, but the correct hand pairs a specific board card that A 6 doesn't. Look at which hole card ranks actually appear on the board.
10 J makes a straight here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
With only one pair, 9 J wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
hand.explanation:
Answer: Q 9

Your opponent flopped two pair and bet all three streets with growing sizing. The consistent pressure — especially the larger turn bet — signals a hand strong enough to protect but not quite a monster.
